Interface YamlSerializer

All Known Implementing Classes:
StandardSerializer
public interface YamlSerializer
Interface used by custom serializer implementations. If you are looking into building your own implementation, it is recommended that you read the wiki.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Object
    deserialize(Map<Object,Object> map)
    Attempts to deserialize the given map into a class instance using this serializer.
    Set<Class<?>>
    getSupportedClasses()
    Returns a set of all explicitly defined classes this serializer supports and can (de-)serialize.
    Set<Class<?>>
    getSupportedParentClasses()
    Returns a set of all parent classes (classes, interfaces...) instances of which this serializer supports and can (de-)serialize.
    <T> Map<Object,Object>
    serialize(T object, MapSupplier supplier)
    Attempts to serialize the given object into a map and returns it.

  • Method Details

    • deserialize

      @Nullable Object deserialize(@NotNull Map<Object,Object> map)
      Attempts to deserialize the given map into a class instance using this serializer.

      The given map is a raw object map - there are no Block instances, just the values themselves.

      If the serializer does not recognize the map, or could not deserialize the map, this method must return null.

      Parameters:
      map - the raw map to deserialize
      Returns:
      the deserialized object

    • serialize

      @Nullable <T> Map<Object,Object> serialize(@NotNull T object, @NotNull MapSupplier supplier)
      Attempts to serialize the given object into a map and returns it.

      The given object is guaranteed to be:

      1. of type contained within getSupportedClasses(),
      2. a sub-class of any of getSupportedParentClasses().
      Map supplier is provided to supply default map as configured in GeneralSettings.getDefaultMapSupplier(). Serializers should not use their own map implementations, they should return the map provided by the supplier.

      If could not serialize into a map, this method must return null.

      Type Parameters:
      T - type of the object to serialize
      Parameters:
      object - the object to serialize
      supplier - the supplier used to supply default maps
      Returns:
      the serialized object

    • getSupportedClasses

      @NotNull Set<Class<?>> getSupportedClasses()
      Returns a set of all explicitly defined classes this serializer supports and can (de-)serialize.

      The returned set cannot be null as indicated by the annotation.

      Returns:
      the set of supported classes

    • getSupportedParentClasses

      @NotNull Set<Class<?>> getSupportedParentClasses()
      Returns a set of all parent classes (classes, interfaces...) instances of which this serializer supports and can (de-)serialize.

      The returned set cannot be null as indicated by the annotation.

      Returns:
      the set of supported parent classes
